document.title: Medical device software - Software life cycle processes

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 62304 Edition 1.1 defines medical device software life cycle processes for development, maintenance, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as an international medical device software life cycle process standard, together with quality management and risk management standards, to support compliance activities for medical device software.
keyPoints: Applies to development and maintenance of medical device software when software is itself a medical device or is embedded or integral to a final medical device.
Requires manufacturers to apply a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
Defines software safety classes A, B, and C and ties process requirements to the assigned safety class.
Adds an alternative compliance route for legacy software through risk management activities, gap analysis, gap closure, and a rationale for continued use.
Includes software development, maintenance, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution processes.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
entities.evidence: The International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) is a worldwide organization for standardization comprising all national electrotechnical committees.

entities.name: IEC 62304
entities.evidence: IEC 62304 Edition 1.1 2015-06 CONSOLIDATED VERSION Medical device software - Software life cycle processes

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

entities.name: ISO 13485
entities.evidence: Demonstration of this ability can be by the use of a quality management system that complies with: - ISO 13485 [8]

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: With the publication of IEC 60601-1:2005 + IEC 60601-1:2005 /AMD1:2012, IEC 62304 is now a normative reference of IEC 60601-1

entities.name: ISO Technical Committee 210
entities.evidence: International Standard IEC 62304 has been prepared by a joint working group of subcommittee 62A... and ISO Technical Committee 210

concepts.name: Medical device software
concepts.evidence: SOFTWARE SYSTEM that has been developed for the purpose of being incorporated into the MEDICAL DEVICE being developed or that is intended for use as a MEDICAL DEVICE

concepts.name: Software life cycle processes
concepts.evidence: This standard defines the life cycle requirements for MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE.

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B, or C)

concepts.name: Legacy software
concepts.evidence: LEGACY SOFTWARE MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE which was legally placed on the market and is still marketed today but for which there is insufficient objective evidence

concepts.name: Software risk management process
concepts.evidence: These requirements are summarized and captured in Clause 7 as the software RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS.

concepts.name: Software development process
concepts.evidence: The software development PROCESS consists of a number of ACTIVITIES.

concepts.name: Software maintenance process
concepts.evidence: the software maintenance PROCESS is considered to be as important as the software development PROCESS

concepts.name: Software configuration management process
concepts.evidence: They are the software configuration management PROCESS (Clause 8) and the software problem resolution PROCESS (Clause 9).

concepts.name: Software problem resolution process
concepts.evidence: The software problem resolution PROCESS is a PROCESS for analyzing and resolving the problems

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: RISK MANAGEMENT FILE set of records and other documents, not necessarily contiguous, that are produced by a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS

concepts.name: Traceability
concepts.evidence: TRACEABILITY degree to which a relationship can be established between two or more products of the development PROCESS

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 Edition 1.1 was published as a consolidated version dated 2015-06.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 Edition 1.1 2015-06 CONSOLIDATED VERSION

evidence.claim: The title of IEC 62304 is Medical device software - Software life cycle processes.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical device software - Software life cycle processes

evidence.claim: The consolidated version consists of the first edition from 2006-05 and Amendment 1 from 2015-06.
evidence.evidenceText: It consists of the first edition (2006-05) ... and its amendment 1 (2015-06)

evidence.claim: The consolidated version is not itself an official IEC Standard; only the current standard and amendments are official documents.
evidence.evidenceText: This Consolidated version is not an official IEC Standard... Only the current versions of the standard and its amendment(s) are to be considered the official documents.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 defines life cycle requirements for medical device software.
evidence.evidenceText: This standard defines the life cycle requirements for MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 applies when software is itself a medical device or is embedded or integral to the final medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: This standard applies to the development and maintenance of MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE when software is itself a MEDICAL DEVICE or when software is an embedded or integral part of the final MEDICAL DEVICE.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 does not cover validation and final release of the medical device, even if the device consists entirely of software.
evidence.evidenceText: This standard does not cover validation and final release of the MEDICAL DEVICE, even when the MEDICAL DEVICE consists entirely of software.

evidence.claim: Compliance is defined by implementing all required processes, activities, and tasks according to software safety class.
evidence.evidenceText: Compliance with this standard is defined as implementing all of the PROCESSES, ACTIVITIES, and TASKS identified in this standard in accordance with the software safety class.

evidence.claim: ISO 14971 is the normative reference for IEC 62304 risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971, Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must apply a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Medical device software manufacturers must demonstrate the ability to provide software meeting customer and applicable regulatory requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER of MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE shall demonstrate the ability to provide MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE that consistently meets customer requirements and applicable regulatory requirements.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software safety classes are A, B, and C.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B, or C)

evidence.claim: Class A applies when the software system cannot contribute to a hazardous situation or contributes only where risk is not unacceptable after external risk controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class A if: the SOFTWARE SYSTEM cannot contribute to a HAZARDOUS SITUATION; or ... does not result in unacceptable RISK

evidence.claim: Class B applies when possible harm is non-serious injury after external risk controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class B if... the resulting possible HARM is non-SERIOUS INJURY.

evidence.claim: Class C applies when possible harm is death or serious injury after external risk controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class C if... the resulting possible HARM is death or SERIOUS INJURY.

evidence.claim: Until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements apply.
evidence.evidenceText: For each SOFTWARE SYSTEM, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements shall apply.

evidence.claim: Legacy software compliance may be demonstrated through IEC 62304 clauses 4.4.2 to 4.4.5 instead of applying Clauses 5 through 9.
evidence.evidenceText: As an alternative to applying Clauses 5 through 9 of this standard, compliance of LEGACY SOFTWARE may be demonstrated as indicated in 4.4.2 to 4.4.5.

evidence.claim: Legacy software risk management must assess feedback including post-production information on incidents or near incidents.
evidence.evidenceText: assess any feedback, including post-production information, on LEGACY SOFTWARE regarding incidents and / or near incidents

evidence.claim: Legacy software gap analysis compares available deliverables against requirements in 5.2, 5.3, 5.7, and Clause 7.
evidence.evidenceText: perform a gap analysis of available DELIVERABLES against those required according to 5.2, 5.3, 5.7, and Clause 7.

evidence.claim: The minimum deliverable for legacy software gap analysis is software system test records.
evidence.evidenceText: The minimum DELIVERABLE shall be SOFTWARE SYSTEM test records (see 5.7.5).

evidence.claim: Software development planning must include traceability among system requirements, software requirements, software system tests, and software risk control measures.
evidence.evidenceText: TRACEABILITY between SYSTEM requirements, software requirements, SOFTWARE SYSTEM test, and RISK CONTROL measures implemented in software

evidence.claim: Software requirements must include regulatory requirements where appropriate.
evidence.evidenceText: As appropriate to the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE, the MANUFACTURER shall include in the software requirements... l) regulatory requirements.

evidence.claim: Risk control measures implemented in software must be included in software requirements for Class B and C software.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall include RISK CONTROL measures implemented in software in the requirements as appropriate to the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE. [Class B, C]

evidence.claim: Software architecture must describe the software structure and identify software items for Class B and C.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall transform the requirements for the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE into a documented ARCHITECTURE that describes the software’s structure and identifies the SOFTWARE ITEMS. [Class B, C]

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must document title, manufacturer, and unique SOUP designator for each SOUP configuration item.
evidence.evidenceText: For each SOUP CONFIGURATION ITEM being used... the MANUFACTURER shall document: a) the title, b) the MANUFACTURER, and c) the unique SOUP designator

evidence.claim: Software maintenance planning must address feedback after release and use of risk management, problem resolution, and configuration management processes.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall establish a software maintenance plan... feedback arising after release... use of the software RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS... software problem resolution PROCESS... software configuration management PROCESS

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must inform users and regulators about released software problems and available changes as required by local regulation.
evidence.evidenceText: As required by local regulation, the MANUFACTURER shall inform users and regulators about: any problem in released MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE... and the nature of any available changes

evidence.claim: Software risk management requires identifying software items that could contribute to hazardous situations from the medical device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall identify SOFTWARE ITEMS that could contribute to a hazardous situation identified in the MEDICAL DEVICE RISK ANALYSIS ACTIVITY of ISO 14971

evidence.claim: Software risk management traceability links hazardous situation, software item, software cause, risk control measure, and verification.
evidence.evidenceText: document TRACEABILITY... from the hazardous situation to the SOFTWARE ITEM; from the SOFTWARE ITEM to the specific software cause; from the software cause to the RISK CONTROL measure; and from the RISK CONTROL measure to the VERIFICATION

evidence.claim: Software configuration management requires unique identification of configuration items and versions.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall establish a scheme for the unique identification of CONFIGURATION ITEMS and their VERSIONS

evidence.claim: Changes to controlled configuration items may occur only in response to an approved change request.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall change CONFIGURATION ITEMS identified to be controlled according to 8.1 only in response to an approved CHANGE REQUEST.

evidence.claim: Problem reports must include a statement of criticality such as effect on performance, safety, or security.
evidence.evidenceText: PROBLEM REPORTS shall include a statement of criticality (for example, effect on performance, SAFETY, or SECURITY)

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, but the document text identifies IEC as the issuing standards organization; FDA appears only in bibliography references.
Product model metadata is absent and productSignal is Unclassified; productModels left empty.
Preprocessed text includes both Redline and Final versions plus repeated license/OCR artifacts; extracted facts prefer the Final version where wording differs.
The document states the consolidated version is not an official IEC Standard, while also presenting IEC 62304 as an International Standard; documentType reflects consolidated-version status.
